title: Introduce shared ThreadPoolExecutor primitive (autocue.analysis.concurrency)
description: Create a single shared thread-pool primitive used by every analysis fanout (generate-apply, health, classify, auto-tag, enrich, similar index build). Centralizes pool size config so the operator has one knob.
acceptanceCriteria: Module autocue/analysis/concurrency.py exists and exports get_pool() and pool_size().
Default pool size resolves to min(8, os.cpu_count() or 1).
Environment variable AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE overrides default; values <1 are clamped to 1; non-int raises ValueError at import-not at use.
Module exposes shutdown_pool() for clean test teardown.
Unit test in tests/test_concurrency.py covers default, override, clamp, singleton, and shutdown.
No new runtime dependency is introduced (stdlib only).

steps.details: New file. Imports: os, threading, concurrent.futures. Module-level _pool = None, _pool_lock = threading.Lock(). Functions: pool_size() reads AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE env, falls back to min(8, os.cpu_count() or 1), clamps to max(1, n). get_pool() acquires _pool_lock; if _pool is None, sets _pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=pool_size(), thread_name_prefix='autocue-pool'); returns _pool. shutdown_pool() acquires lock; if _pool not None, calls _pool.shutdown(wait=True, cancel_futures=False); sets _pool=None.

Test cases: (a) default size equals min(8, cpu_count or 1) when env unset; (b) AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE='4' yields 4 (use monkeypatch.setenv + shutdown_pool between cases); (c) AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE='0' clamps to 1; (d) AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE='abc' raises ValueError on pool_size() call; (e) get_pool() returns the same instance on repeated calls; (f) shutdown_pool() clears the singleton so the next get_pool() returns a fresh executor. Use pytest fixture autouse=True calling shutdown_pool() after each test.

In serve/deps.py lifespan() (or app shutdown hook in serve/app.py), call shutdown_pool() during the existing app shutdown path so the pool is closed on uvicorn stop. Do NOT pre-create the pool at startup-let lazy init handle first use.

steps.description: Document the env var in CLAUDE.md
steps.details: Add a single line under the 'Development commands' section: 'export AUTOCUE_POOL_SIZE=8  # override default pool size for parallel analysis loops'. Also note in the 'Must-know constraints' table that the pool serves I/O-bound work; the writer side of any DB mutation must stay single-threaded.

steps.description: Run the full Python test suite
steps.details: pytest -q. Verify all 819+ existing tests still green and the 6 new concurrency tests green. No flakiness over 5 consecutive runs.

technicalNotes: ThreadPoolExecutor is lazy-init: don't fork a pool at module import-tests that touch env vars need a fresh pool per case.
thread_name_prefix='autocue-pool' makes traces and py-spy output legible.
shutdown(wait=True) blocks until in-flight work finishes; use cancel_futures=False to preserve in-flight SSE writes.
